entities.name: pegvaliase
entities.context: Pegvaliase is a pegylated PAL enzyme that converts phenylalanine to ammonia and trans-cinnamic acid.

entities.name: phenylalanine ammonia lyase
entities.context: Searches of MEDLINE (1946-September 1, 2018) were conducted using the terms pegvaliase and phenylalanine ammonia lyase (PAL).

entities.name: phenylalanine
entities.context: a phenylalanine-metabolizing enzyme indicated to reduce blood phenylalanine concentrations

entities.name: ammonia
entities.context: pegylated PAL enzyme that converts phenylalanine to ammonia and trans-cinnamic acid

entities.name: trans-cinnamic acid
entities.context: pegylated PAL enzyme that converts phenylalanine to ammonia and trans-cinnamic acid

entities.name: phenylketonuria
entities.context: The mainstay of therapy in phenylketonuria (PKU) has historically consisted of dietary restriction of phenylalanine.

entities.name: enzyme substitution therapy
entities.context: Pegvaliase injection is the first Food and Drug Administration (FDA)-approved enzyme substitution therapy for patients with PKU.

entities.name: elevated blood phenylalanine
entities.context: patients with documented blood phenylalanine >600 µmol/L who have failed existing management strategies

entities.name: dietary phenylalanine restriction
entities.context: The mainstay of therapy in phenylketonuria (PKU) has historically consisted of dietary restriction of phenylalanine.

entities.name: Food and Drug Administration
entities.context: Pegvaliase injection is the first Food and Drug Administration (FDA)-approved enzyme substitution therapy for patients with PKU.
